inputPackage.rule: A standardized Wiki datasource package has not been defined yet. Do not consume raw crawled data directly and do not invent field paths in this mapping.

rules: Only bind data that exists in a confirmed Wiki datasource or development-approved mock.
Do not invent category, entry, route, image, badge, count, progress, attribute, table, or rich-text fields.
Use TODO_FROM_DATASOURCE for every source path until the Wiki datasource contract is confirmed.
Use the shared component registry at skill-pack/components.json. Wiki does not maintain a separate component registry.
Use only components allowed by the target region in skill-pack/wiki/templates/wiki.json.
Keep the default Wiki canvas at 1000 x 610.
Keep mobile mapping undefined until mobile Wiki design nodes or explicit implementation constraints are supplied.
Missing optional fields should collapse the related UI element instead of showing fabricated placeholder content.
Breadcrumb labels and target routes must come from datasource or routing context.
Pagination state must come from datasource or renderer state, not from hardcoded sample values.
Home summary title follows the WikiHome two-segment title row: '<game name>' followed by the literal 'Wiki攻略'.

Home page uses Figma node 477:3685. Bind the 936 x 100 summary banner image from the tool/game config when provided; in preview or missing-data states use the configured placeholder only and do not invent a final game/tool image.
Nav.TopBar items are global first-level Wiki categories.
Form.SearchBar is global Wiki search across categories and entries.
For Game.CategoryCard in home.category-grid, filter out categories whose linked secondary page or category total item count is 0.

For Game.CategoryCard in home.category-grid, avatarSrcs must preview up to the first three items of the linked secondary page in display order, using item icon first or item image when no icon exists.
For Game.CategoryCard in home.category-grid, countLabel must represent the total item count for the linked secondary page or category, not the avatar preview count.

For card-like components such as Game.CategoryCard, Game.ShowCard, and Layout.DescribeCard, enable hover styling only when the card has a real target route, drill-down, modal/detail opener, playable action, expansion, or another action that reveals new information. If the card only displays static information and cannot click or trigger anything new, keep interactive=false and do not render hover state.
Treat template pages as placement references, not datasource shape requirements. Referencing a template page does not require rendering every observed region or component in that frame.
Select the component family from the original source page structure and information density first, then apply the closest matching template layout for that component family.

Use Base.Modal through modal.item-explanation only when the original source page opens a modal overlay to explain a single selected item or video source material. Bind variant=detail with title, subtitle, description, imageSrc, and open state for text/image explanation modals. Bind variant=video with videoSrc, posterSrc, videoTitle, and open state for video media modals. Do not use Modal for normal detail pages, tooltip text disclosure, card summaries, or map popup content. Modal chrome is fixed by the component: detail panel max-height 484, description max-height 256, #11161B background, and internal scrollbar uses Layout.Scroll visual style.

For any Wiki card set that renders imageSrc, inspect source asset dimensions or visual content for the current rendered page/list and bind imageRatio to the closest supported Base.Image ratio before rendering. Use 1:1 for square or near-square assets, 4:5 for vertical portraits/card art/posters around width/height 0.8, 3:2 for moderately wide assets, and 16:9 for wide landscape/banners/maps/screenshots/weapons. Keep one ratio within a current page/list when visual consistency is needed, but re-evaluate imageRatio independently for each sibling second-level tab or secondary page. Do not reuse a 3:2, 4:5, or 16:9 decision from one tab on another tab whose source images are square or near-square.

When a secondary page renders no second-level navigation, no third-level navigation/sidebar, and no breadcrumbs above its list, place the list region 20px from the Body top. Do not inherit the lower list y offset from templates that reserve space for breadcrumbs or secondary navigation.

In secondary.card-list, choose Game.ShowCard only when every visible entry's subtitle/body text in the current rendered page/list fits within one visible line and the card can fully represent the original source entry; choose compact Layout.DescribeCard when the current page/list needs richer inline description, image, badge, or meta fields; choose the SecondaryPageLargeCard Layout.DescribeCard grid only when the current source page is image-first or gallery-like.

If any source card in the current secondary, multi-level, or related rendered page/list needs more than one visible line of secondary/rich text, use Layout.DescribeCard with size=SM for all related cards in that current page/list instead of mixing Game.ShowCard with DescribeCard. Bind the subtitle/body text to the SM description node and let the card/description height adapt to the rendered text; expose the full string through Base.ToolTip only when a selected template or host container actually truncates or hides the text. Do not carry this decision across sibling second-level tabs or the whole first-level category.

For Layout.DescribeCard image data, bind imageSrc and imageRatio to the nested Base.Image/ImageFrame component inside the DescribeCard. Do not create or map a separate raw image element outside the nested component.

For Layout.DescribeCard, derive showImage, showTitle, showDescription, showBadge, and showAttributes from the actual source fields and presentation requirement. Set a show flag false when the source lacks that field or the selected template intentionally omits that region; false means the region is not rendered and does not keep placeholder spacing.

Using SecondaryPageLargeCard means using its large-card placement rules for chosen Layout.DescribeCard entries, not forcing all source pages into large image cards. For card-like large grids without third-level navigation, use repeat(4, 210px) by default and downgrade to repeat(3, 210px) only when the available width cannot fit four columns plus gaps. When the chosen large-card entries render only one row, vertically center that row inside the large-card list container after choosing the column template; item count or single-row state must not change the column count by itself.
Use secondary.multi-level-tabs only when the original source page exposes second-level branches under the active first-level Nav.TopBar category.

Use secondary.multi-level-sidebar only when the active secondary tab exposes real third-level groups or local subcategory anchors. If the source page has only second-level navigation, omit the sidebar and do not fabricate third-level items.

Use secondary.multi-level-card-list only when the entries under the active second-level tab and optional active third-level group are best represented by the observed two-column Game.ShowCard list and every entry subtitle/body fits within one visible line. Otherwise keep the matched navigation placement and choose the content component that best fits the original page structure.

For Game.ShowCard, derive targetRoute or hasNextLevel from the source hierarchy. If the represented entry is terminal and has no next page, set hasNextLevel=false or showRightIcon=false so the right arrow is hidden.

For playable audio entries, use Game.ShowCard with variant=voice. Bind audioSrc from the audio asset URL/id, durationLabel from source metadata or probed audio duration, currentTimeLabel/progress/isPlaying from renderer playback state, and keep showRightIcon=false unless the audio entry also has a separate real detail route.

For Game.ShowCard descriptions, pass the full subtitle/body text even though the component visibly clamps to one line; the full text must be exposed through Base.ToolTip only when the visible text is actually truncated or hidden, with a short hover delay and auto placement that avoids viewport clipping. Render the ToolTip bubble through a page/body-level portal overlay rather than inside the card/list row so overflow:hidden, clip, or scroll containers cannot crop it.

Breadcrumbs are only for final detail pages. Each breadcrumb label is clickable and maps to the route for that hierarchy level. Each Nav.Breadcrumbs item must hug its rendered label text width rather than using a fixed sample width; current breadcrumb component frame height is 26px and text uses 12px/18px.
Detail hero card component family follows the card family used on the previous page for the entry that opened the detail page.

DetailContent is a fixed template shell, not data content: preserve its background #000306 / var(--color-bg-black), 1px inside rgba(255,255,255,0.10) / var(--color-stroke-area) stroke, radius 8, clipsContent=true, padding=0, and gap=0. HeroSection uses padding=16 and gap=10; DetailSections/ExpandedSections use padding=16 and vertical gap=12.

Use guide.document-detail for document/guide article pages with long prose, one or more article media blocks, article metadata, optional local anchors, and optional related-info links. Choose GuideDetailWithNavAndInfo when both local anchors and related-info links exist, GuideDetailWithInfo when only related-info links exist, and GuideDetailWithNav when only local anchors exist. Do not fabricate either side module when the source lacks it.

When guide.document-detail renders a right RelatedInfoSidebar, keep the template chrome even though it is not source data: title frame h=36 with a 1px bottom divider at y=36, w=260, using rgba(255,255,255,0.10) / var(--color-stroke-area); related items still start at y=44.

For guide.document-detail article images, keep each datasource image's original aspect ratio and constrain only max rendered height to 230px. Do not apply card imageRatio rules or force the Figma sample 16:9 ratio to article images.
For detail.attribute-section badges, keep one stable tone for each source tag class and use different tones for different classes when source colors are unavailable.
For detail.extended-section, render content only when the original source structure matches supported rich text, media, table, or extra section blocks.

Detail tables must use structured tableColumns and tableRows when available; do not encode table data as plain rich text. Repeat Data.TableHeaderCell once per source column and Data.TableRowCell once per source row/column cell. Do not hardcode the table to a fixed number of columns or rows. Use compressible column tracks, keep the table within its parent width, and do not add fixed 928px width or horizontal scrolling for character detail intimacy tables.
Layout.DetailCard title decoration is fixed component chrome: render the 1.5 x 14 primary-color marker before the title text and do not require or bind any datasource field for it.

When mapping detail sections to Layout.DetailCard, treat each source section as one possible module. Each rendered module must bind title and at least one real optional block; choose description, features, infoItems, statusBadges, tableColumns/tableRows/table, aside, media, or child content from the source section shape. Preserve source section order for module stacking, but use the template detail layout for spacing, width, gaps, and scroll behavior.

fieldMappings.source: TODO_FROM_DATASOURCE_OR_TOOL_CONFIG > home summary banner image for the current tool/game; preview fallback may use placeholder
fieldMappings.rule: The user supplies different tool/game images. Use the provided image when available; otherwise keep the configured placeholder for preview only.

fieldMappings.source: TODO_FROM_DATASOURCE > source modal state for selected item explanation or video media, including modal type, title, optional subtitle, full description, optional icon/image, optional videoSrc/posterSrc/videoTitle, and open/close renderer state
fieldMappings.rule: Bind only when the original source page actually represents the item explanation or video media as a modal overlay. Use variant=detail for text/image explanation and variant=video for video source material. The detail lower description area receives the full text and scrolls internally if content exceeds its max height.

fieldMappings.source: TODO_FROM_DATASOURCE > game name composed as '<game name>' + fixed 'Wiki攻略' suffix, visible first-level category count, and total item count
TODO_FROM_DATASOURCE > top-level categories with total item count greater than 0
TODO_FROM_DATASOURCE > each visible top-level category > first three linked secondary-page items > icon or image asset URLs in display order, preferring icon when both icon and image exist
TODO_FROM_DATASOURCE > each visible top-level category > total item count for the linked secondary page or category
TODO_FROM_DATASOURCE_OR_ROUTING_CONTEXT > each visible top-level category > linked secondary-page route
TODO_FROM_DATASOURCE_OR_ROUTING_CONTEXT > global first-level Wiki categories and active first-level category
TODO_FROM_DATASOURCE_OR_RENDERER_STATE > global Wiki search query

TODO_FROM_DATASOURCE > entries for the current rendered secondary page/list only, plus routing hierarchy and image asset dimensions/content type from those visible entries. Choose card family and imageRatio for this current page/list: Game.ShowCard only when all visible entry subtitle/body text fits one visible line, compact Layout.DescribeCard size=SM when any visible entry needs more than one line or richer inline fields, or SecondaryPageLargeCard-style Layout.DescribeCard when the current source page structure is image-first. Do not include sibling second-level tabs when deriving these choices.
fieldMappings.target: regions[secondary.card-list].components[Game.ShowCard or Layout.DescribeCard].dataBinding.entries, currentPageList.imageRatio or entries[].imageRatio derived only from the current page/list, selected card family, selected layout variant including Game.ShowCard variant=voice for playable audio entries, and Layout.DescribeCard size=SM plus showImage/showTitle/showDescription/showBadge/showAttributes when replacing ShowCard for richer text

fieldMappings.source: TODO_FROM_DATASOURCE_OR_RENDERER_STATE > pagination current page from renderer or route, totalPages from backend
TODO_FROM_DATASOURCE_OR_ROUTING_CONTEXT > active first-level category > second-level navigation branches
TODO_FROM_DATASOURCE_OR_ROUTING_CONTEXT > active second-level branch > third-level groups or local anchors plus active third-level id

TODO_FROM_DATASOURCE > entries filtered by active first-level category, active second-level tab, optional active third-level group, per-entry next-level route availability, and page/list-level check that every entry subtitle/body fits one visible line before using Game.ShowCard
fieldMappings.target: regions[secondary.multi-level-card-list].components[Game.ShowCard or Layout.DescribeCard].dataBinding.entries and selected card family

fieldMappings.source: TODO_FROM_DATASOURCE_OR_ROUTING_CONTEXT > final detail page hierarchy labels and target routes
RENDERER_SCROLL_STATE > real detail page scroll position, scroll height, viewport height, and source/template scrollbar placement including actual visual trackHeight
TODO_FROM_DATASOURCE_OR_ROUTING_CONTEXT > selected detail summary and previous-page card family
TODO_FROM_DATASOURCE > selected detail attributes, source tag classes, source colors when available, and badge labels
TODO_FROM_DATASOURCE > related entries and progress values
TODO_FROM_DATASOURCE > structured detail sections and tables
TODO_FROM_DATASOURCE > extended rich text, media, additional tables, or extra sections that match supported detail blocks

TODO_FROM_DATASOURCE > article-like guide detail page: breadcrumb path, article title, article metadata, main media, paragraphs/rich blocks, optional local section anchors, and optional related-info links
